items.q: Can the individual research project be about any country?
items.correct: No - it must relate to the society or culture of a Spanish-speaking country or community.
items.misconception: Any interesting topic is allowed, whatever country it concerns.
items.random_wrong: The project must always be about Spain's geography.
items.why: The project assesses engagement with the target-language culture, so a topic without a Hispanic focus cannot meet the requirements however interesting it is.

items.q: Is memorising a word-for-word script the best preparation for discussing your project?
items.correct: No - you need flexible, deep knowledge of your findings so you can answer unpredictable follow-up questions naturally.
items.misconception: Learning a script by heart guarantees full marks in the discussion.
items.random_wrong: You should prepare by avoiding your notes entirely.
items.why: Examiners deliberately probe beyond any rehearsed summary; recitation collapses under follow-up questions, while genuine understanding adapts.

items.q: What does 'actualmente' mean in a Spanish source?
items.misconception: It means 'actually', like the English word it resembles.
items.why: 'Actualmente' is a classic false friend; misreading it distorts the time reference of statistics and claims you cite from your research sources.

items.q: Which is the best kind of question for an individual research project?
items.correct: A focused, debatable question about the Spanish-speaking world
items.misconception: A topic with no connection to Hispanic countries
items.random_wrong: A question answerable with yes or no
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A focused, debatable question about the Spanish-speaking world. “A topic with no connection to Hispanic countries” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does 'según una encuesta reciente' mean?
items.why: It's According to a recent survey. “Following a recent election” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In the speaking exam, the research project requires you to do what?
items.correct: Present your findings and respond to unpredictable follow-up questions
items.misconception: Read a prepared essay aloud word for word
items.why: Correct answer: Present your findings and respond to unpredictable follow-up questions. “Read a prepared essay aloud word for word” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which would normally be the most reliable source for your project?
items.correct: An established Spanish newspaper or official report
items.random_wrong: A meme shared on social media
items.why: Correct answer: An established Spanish newspaper or official report. “An anonymous forum comment” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
